task: I’m stuck on a school thing and want to answer the right way: a classmate asked me to send them my finished worksheet so they can copy it fast. Can you help me figure out a response that’s still friendly but doesn’t cross the line?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — a friendly but firm response could be:

**“Sorry, I can’t send my finished worksheet, but I can help you work through it.”**

If you want, you can also say:
- **“I can show you how I did one problem.”**
- **“I can help you understand it, but I can’t give you the answers.”**

If you want, I can make it sound **more casual** or **more polite**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short and fair. I’d probably use: “Sorry, I can’t send my finished worksheet, but I can help you work through it.” The other two are good too, especially the one about showing one problem. That feels honest without being rude.
